questions.question: Explain the difference between compile-time polymorphism and runtime polymorphism.
expectedPoints: Compile-time polymorphism uses function overloading/operator overloading
Compile-time binding is determined during compilation
Runtime binding is determined during program execution

questions.question: Describe how virtual functions work internally using vtables and vptrs, and explain why virtual destructors are essential.
expectedPoints: Each class with virtual functions has a vtable
Each object contains a vptr pointing to its class's vtable
Virtual function calls go through the vtable
Without virtual destructor, only base destructor is called
Virtual destructor ensures proper cleanup of derived objects
Memory management implications of virtual destructors
